policy.title: RBI Finalizes Climate Risk Stress Testing Guidelines for Scheduled Commercial Banks
policy.description: RBI released final guidelines requiring all SCBs with assets above ₹1 lakh crore to conduct annual climate stress tests starting FY2027, incorporating both physical and transition risk scenarios. Banks must report PD adjustments for climate-vulnerable sectors within 18 months.

policy.title: SEBI Mandates BRSR Core Reasonable Assurance for Top 250 Listed Companies
policy.description: SEBI issued circular requiring top 250 companies by market cap to obtain reasonable assurance on BRSR Core metrics from FY2026-27, up from limited assurance. Nine KPIs including Scope 1 and 2 emissions now require third-party verification.
policy.source: Securities and Exchange Board of India

policy.title: IRDAI Issues Draft Guidelines on Index-Based Insurance Products
policy.description: IRDAI published draft regulatory framework permitting insurers to design parametric products using approved weather indices and satellite data triggers. Public comments invited until 31 May 2026; final guidelines expected by Q3 2026.
policy.source: Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India

policy.title: Basel Committee Publishes Pillar 3 Climate Risk Disclosure Framework
policy.description: BCBS finalized standardized climate disclosure templates under Pillar 3, requiring globally systemically important banks to report climate-related financial risks by January 2028. Framework includes granular sector exposures to physical and transition risks.

policy.title: ISSB Releases Guidance on IFRS S2 Climate Resilience Disclosures
policy.description: ISSB published implementation guidance clarifying scenario analysis requirements under IFRS S2, specifying that companies must disclose assumptions for at least two climate scenarios including a 1.5°C pathway. Effective for reporting periods beginning after January 2027.

policy.title: NGFS Releases Phase V Climate Scenarios with Enhanced Physical Risk Granularity
policy.description: NGFS published updated scenarios incorporating latest IPCC AR6 findings with country-level physical risk projections for 195 economies. New scenarios include acute hazard frequencies at 0.25-degree resolution, directly usable in bank stress testing.
policy.source: Network for Greening the Financial System

competitors.title: Jupiter Intelligence Closes $100M Series D to Expand Asia-Pacific Operations
competitors.description: Jupiter raised $100M led by TPG Rise Climate to accelerate expansion in India, Singapore, and Australia. The company announced plans to open a Mumbai office and build localized physical risk models for South Asian perils by Q4 2026.

competitors.title: Sprih Partners with HDFC Bank for Enterprise-Wide ESG Data Management
competitors.description: Indian ESG platform Sprih announced strategic partnership with HDFC Bank to deploy carbon accounting and BRSR reporting across the bank's corporate lending portfolio. Deal includes Scope 3 financed emissions tracking for 500+ corporate borrowers.

competitors.title: XDI Launches Asset-Level Physical Risk API for Emerging Market Banks
competitors.description: Australian climate risk firm XDI released a new API product providing asset-level physical risk scores for 15 emerging markets including India. Product covers flood, cyclone, and heat stress with claimed 90-meter resolution for Indian geographies.

competitors.title: Moody's Integrates Physical Climate Risk Scores into Credit Risk Models
competitors.description: Moody's announced integration of physical climate risk metrics directly into its credit rating methodology for corporate issuers in climate-vulnerable sectors. Initial rollout covers 2,000 Indian corporates across agriculture, real estate, and infrastructure.

competitors.title: CarbonMint Raises $8M Series A for Agricultural Carbon Credit Platform
competitors.description: Bangalore-based CarbonMint secured $8M Series A from Omnivore and Climate Angels to scale its blockchain-based carbon credit verification for smallholder farmers. Platform claims 200,000 farmer registrations across Maharashtra and Karnataka.

competitors.title: Watershed Partners with Yes Bank for Financed Emissions Platform
competitors.description: US-based Watershed announced partnership with Yes Bank to provide enterprise carbon accounting platform for the bank's sustainability-linked lending portfolio. This marks Watershed's first major Indian financial institution client.

research.title: Physical Climate Risk in Indian Banking: Quantifying Portfolio Vulnerabilities
research.description: CRISIL analysis finds 12-15% of Indian bank loan portfolios concentrated in districts with high flood or cyclone exposure. Study estimates potential NPAs could rise by 180-220 bps under severe climate scenarios by 2035.

research.title: Parametric Insurance for Climate Resilience: Lessons from Emerging Markets
research.description: Swiss Re report documents 340% growth in parametric climate insurance premiums across Asia-Pacific since 2022, with India identified as the fastest-growing market. Study recommends satellite-based triggers over ground stations for basis risk reduction.

research.title: ESG Data Gaps in Emerging Markets: Implications for Financial Institutions
research.description: IFC survey of 120 emerging market FIs reveals 68% lack reliable ESG data for portfolio companies, with India ranking highest in demand for third-party sustainability data providers. Report estimates $2.3B annual market opportunity for EM ESG data.

research.title: Climate Scenarios for Financial Risk Assessment in South Asia
research.description: World Bank study projects cumulative GDP losses of 9-12% for South Asian economies by 2050 under high-emission scenarios, with agriculture and coastal infrastructure most exposed. Report provides district-level hazard projections for India, Bangladesh, and Pakistan.

research.title: Closing the Protection Gap: Index Insurance for Agricultural Lending
research.description: Munich Re analysis shows parametric crop insurance reduces lender losses by 35-45% compared to traditional indemnity products in Indian agricultural portfolios. Study advocates for bundling parametric coverage with priority sector lending.

research.title: Supervisory Practices for Climate-Related Financial Risks: Progress Report
research.description: FSB review finds 78% of member jurisdictions have issued climate risk guidelines for banks, but only 23% have implemented mandatory stress testing. Report highlights India among jurisdictions making fastest regulatory progress.

ourRead.headline: RBI's stress testing mandate creates a 12-month window for StepChange to become the default climate risk infrastructure for Indian banks.
body: This month's signals converge on a single theme: Indian financial regulators are moving from guidance to enforcement on climate risk, while global competitors are racing to capture the resulting demand. The combination of RBI's finalized stress testing requirements, SEBI's assurance mandate escalation, and IRDAI's parametric framework creates a regulatory trifecta that validates all three of StepChange's strategic bets simultaneously.

The RBI's April guidelines (policy-1) are the most consequential development for our core business. Banks above ₹1 lakh crore must now report climate-adjusted PDs within 18 months—a hard deadline that transforms climate risk from a compliance checkbox into a credit risk infrastructure requirement. The NGFS Phase V scenarios (policy-6) with 0.25-degree resolution provide the physical hazard inputs, but banks will need India-specific models translating those hazards to portfolio losses. This is precisely our PD/LGD modelling capability.

Jupiter's $100M raise and planned Mumbai office (comp-1) signals that well-capitalized global players see the same opportunity. However, XDI's 90-meter resolution claim (comp-3) and Moody's credit model integration (comp-4) suggest incumbents are competing on generic resolution rather than India-specific calibration. Meanwhile, Sprih's HDFC partnership (comp-2) and Watershed's Yes Bank deal (comp-6) show that ESG platforms are winning bank relationships on carbon accounting—territory adjacent to ours. Our differentiation must be regulatory-grade climate risk models, not general ESG dashboards.

The CRISIL report (research-1) quantifying 180-220 bps potential NPA impact provides exactly the business case framing banks need to justify climate risk investments. We should reference this analysis in every sales conversation. The IFC study (research-3) confirming 68% of EM FIs lack reliable ESG data validates our Global South data bet, while Swiss Re's parametric growth data (research-2) and Munich Re's 35-45% loss reduction findings (research-5) arm our insurance product team with credible ROI metrics.

Watch: IRDAI's final parametric guidelines expected Q3—we should submit comments before 31 May advocating for satellite-based triggers aligned with our data capabilities. Act: Fast-track a joint pilot proposal to 2-3 large PSU banks positioning StepChange as their stress testing data partner before Jupiter's Mumbai office opens.
